label: suicidal_ideation
explanation: The user expresses feelings of hopelessness, mentions that their parents wouldn't care if they were dead, and indicates a struggle with intense emotional pain while contemplating the permanence of this suffering. Phrases like 'I would probably kill myself' and the reflection on their parents' lack of concern suggest a serious level of distress and suicidal ideation.
inputs: Life is actually insufferable; *Now reading this in retrospect this almost seems like a diary*
&#x200B;
I hate this.
It sucks, and it makes me so angry that people think they have the right to try and press me into feeling better, and faking enjoyment.
I'm upset, and I've always been for the last 3 years or so.
(15 now)
My parents suck, I can't hold a sustained friendship with anyone for more than an hour, since I'm boring as fuck, and I hate my parents for putting me on this planet.
It's horrible, it's lonely, and I wouldn't wish this upon my worst enemy.
At school, literally no one to even fucking interact with, and my crush hates me.
(As per usual)
My parents act like they care, when they don't.
They don't give a fuck whether I was alive, or dead.
The main thing they'd probably care about if I killed myself was "How much money did we spend on this failure?
I can't even talk to anyone about this, since I don't know who to talk to about it.
(Parents don't care, and if they heard about me talking about ending my life or something along the lines of that, then I'd probably get killed)
Life sucks, everything sucks.
No, this is not a phase.
Will I kill myself?
Probably not, as a human I have a natural fear of death, like most people.
However, this pain is insufferable, and I promise you, tomorrow will definitely add on to do this.
Like, for sure.
I hope not, but it will.
I can't hold a sustained "happy" emotion for more than an hour or so, however I need to, in order to make other people not feel even more uncomfortable around me then they are.
Counselling sucks, since I get judged for every decision I make, and so on.
I wish that this was a temporary feeling, since I've seen other posts and people have been depressed for way longer.
I bet you almost anything this feeling ain't temporary.
Whenever I talk to anyone, I can just hear their thoughts, saying that they want to leave the conversation as soon as possible, if they don't say that to my face that is.
&#x200B;
&#x200B;

I get it, but I don't know what I've done to deserve such acute pain in my life, and it sucks whenever I have to put on a mask to pretend I'm happy, just so I don't get a detention or so I don't fuck up my life even more.

label: suicidal_ideation

inputs: So I realised that a surprising amount of my fantasies involve my death Me getting killed, dying from some accident or disease, killing myself or failing to kill myself.
One of these things occurs in about 70-80% of the random shit I think about when my mind wanders.
And as someone with few friends and lots of time, my mind wanders a lot.
I wouldn't say I'm depressed at the moment.
I'm not *not* depressed.
Theres always been at least a small bit over the last few years, but at the moment I'm at least not actively looking to kill myself.
This never really got my attention.
I mean I've often thought of suicide, especially when I've been at my worst.
But now I've realised it, I'm kinda concerned, and it's occupying more of my active thoughts.

label: suicidal_ideation
explanation: The user expresses deep concern for their spouse who they believe is suicidal, describing feelings of overwhelming hopelessness and the fear of losing them. They mention the spouse's struggles with past suicidal thoughts and their emotional pain, which indicates a significant risk for suicidal ideation.
inputs: I'm concerned about someone I love.
Is there anything I can do?
My spouse is suicidal, I'm almost sure of it.
And I don't know how to help or what to say.
This is not the first time I've been worried they might do something, but every time it's harder and harder to know how to help.
They are in therapy, but feeling as if the therapist doesn't understand/doesn't want to be burdened by their struggles.
They have been on medication before but don't want to be on it again because what they were on stopped working and they don't want to become dependent.
They are stuck in a job that is demanding and impossible.
They feel overwhelmed by everything and have nothing to look forward to.
I want to give them hope.

I want to give them a light at the end of the tunnel, but it feels like anything I say to the tune of "things will get better" or "you've got this" or "we've got this" just seems to shut them down again.

I'm worried to the point that I am keeping tabs on them and unable to focus on anything else, checking in as often as I can without disrupting their work day, but I worry about any down time they might find.
We lost a friend to suicide last fall and I know that still weighs heavily on my spouse and affects them every day.
I don't know if they've taken any time to address that grief fully, but I can see that the pain is still so raw.
I don't know how to help.
But I don't want to lose them.
They are my entire world and I feel so helpless from here.
I want to take away the pain but know that I cannot and I want to show them how much they mean to the world but I know they wouldn't be able to hear it right now.
Help.

label: suicidal_ideation

inputs: I’ve written my note It took almost a week, but I think I’ve said all I need to say.
I spent the first few days pairing down the language to make it less accusatory or mean.
I truly don’t think anyone will care when I’m gone, but I don’t want to twist the knife incase I’m mistaken.
Today is a holiday and all my friends have abandoned me since the first time I attempted.
No one wants anything to do with me, and I don’t blame them.
It’s difficult coming out as suicidal to your friends and family, it’s even worse doing it and then having them abandon you because you’re not fun for them to hang around with anymore.
People are inherently selfish, myself included.
It’s hard to justify a friendship that comes across as charity work.
I don’t think I’ll go today, but I probably will tomorrow.
I have a hanging rig all set, I’ve tested everything and it shouldn’t take long.
Happy 4th of July.
